message:
docs(ft-og9bi): document atomic-redeploy constraint for codec wire protocol

Closes the codec-docs trio (ft-k6jco proposal, ft-8smkj CI guard,
ft-og9bi runbook). New file docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md gives
operators the runbook for the current state of the protocol —
CODEC_VERSION as a single scalar over a varbincode positional format
means every bump is an atomic redeploy event until ft-kuxho/B's
CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED window lands.

Sections:
  §1 failure mode — why mismatched versions corrupt or panic
  §2 long-lived TLS session caveat — ft-nyvyl bounded handshakes but
     post-handshake sessions are unlimited; rolling upgrades drop them
  §3 operator runbook — pre-deploy checklist, server-first deploy
     order, cross-deploy coordination notes (mux server + clients
     must move together; recorder/replay artifacts unaffected;
     workflows survive via checkpoint subsystem), rollback procedure
  §4 the future relief valve — forward-reference to
     docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md
     and the additive-vs-breaking distinction that determines whether
     the runbook applies
  §5 cross-references to codec-versions.md, the proposal, the CI guard
     script, and the load-bearing source lines

Cross-linked from docs/codec-versions.md as an "operator note" so the
release-notes file points at the runbook for any reader who arrives
via the CI guard's error message.

Closes ft-og9bi.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-o4xi3","title":"Extend proptest_mcp + proptest_mcp_bridge structural invariants","description":"proptest_mcp.rs (3.7KB) and proptest_mcp_bridge.rs (4.0KB) are thin. Add structural protocol invariants: all tool names follow 'wa.' or 'frankenterm.' convention, all resource URIs use a wa:// scheme, all resource templates contain {} placeholders, tool/resource/template sets are stable across rebuilds, initialize response is deterministic for the same config, and the client-side tool list matches the server for multiple suffixes simultaneously. Complements the ft-mejto golden without duplicating it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:38:12.628263Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089459Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089037Z","close_reason":"proptest_mcp_bridge extended with 5 new structural invariants (wa.* namespace, wa:// scheme, resource/template disjointness, build idempotency, non-empty descriptions). proptest_mcp.rs left untouched due to pre-existing test-binary flakiness with >3 proptests (server threads leak).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","proptest","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-o657v","title":"conformance_mcp_manifest: pin tool input_schema additionalProperties:false invariant","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` rejects unknown top-level fields (via `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), checks uniqueness of tool/resource/prompt names, and validates minimal/optional field presence. But it does NOT pin a security-relevant invariant that every shipped MCP tool CURRENTLY satisfies: **every tool's `input_schema` has `additionalProperties: false`**.

Verified by:

    $ rg -c 'additionalProperties' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29
    $ rg -c 'input_schema:' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29

All 29 input_schemas currently set it. Without a test pinning this, a future tool author could add a new tool without the clause and the server would silently accept extra fields from untrusted MCP clients — side-channel surface for policy bypass, audit log pollution, or ambiguous-field attacks on tools that later try to evolve their schema.

# Fix

Add `every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties` in conformance_mcp_manifest.rs that iterates `server.tools()` from `build_server_with_db(&Config::default(), Some(...))` and asserts each `tool.input_schema` is a JSON object with `"additionalProperties": false` at the top level. If a tool's schema is NOT type=object (e.g. `{}`, or a primitive), flag that too — all MCP tools in this server should accept structured object arguments.

# Non-fix

Middleware-injected `format` property (from augment_tool_schema_with_format in mcp_middleware.rs) is a known accepted exception because it's stripped from arguments before the inner call. The test should assert additionalProperties:false; the format injection happens to the properties map, not to additionalProperties, so the test passes cleanly.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect; every current tool sets the bit. But regression risk is real as new tools land, and a silently-missing additionalProperties:false turns into a real policy bypass if the tool ever evolves its allowed field set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:03:57.448008Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683105Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of f73e139a (absorbed commit). every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties iterates server.tools() from build_server_with_db + asserts each input_schema is type=object with additionalProperties literal false — pins the 29/29 invariant so future tools without the clause break CI instead of silently widening the accepted field set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Cross-linked from docs/codec-versions.md operator note (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","codec","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-og9bi","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:58:09.573922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ojgmo","title":"[deadlock] workflow runner cx-cancel can strand on idle event subscriber","description":"File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:769-790; crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:805-810,2157-2170; crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:2159-2167

Repro narrative:
1. Start WorkflowRunner::run_with_cx(...) on an idle EventBus with no detection events flowing.
2. Let the loop pass the top-of-loop cx.checkpoint() and park inside subscriber.recv_cx(cx).
3. Cancel the caller Cx after the recv is already suspended.
4. The underlying asupersync broadcast recv does not register a cancel waker mid-flight, so the parked recv is not re-woken by cancel alone.
5. runtime_compat::broadcast::Receiver::recv_with_cx also collapses Cancelled into Closed, so cancel and bus shutdown are conflated once an external wake finally occurs.
6. Result: the workflow runner shutdown can hang until an unrelated event arrives or the bus closes, which is a real cancellation-stranding bug in the async control path.

Expected: recv_cx should surface Cancelled promptly enough for run_with_cx to stop without waiting for unrelated traffic, and cancel should not be reported as Closed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:38.816705Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:02:09.560010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:02:09.559655Z","close_reason":"Landed in fa1fbffe: event subscriber and runner now surface RecvError::Cancelled and race idle broadcast waits against cx cancel. Targeted verification was partially blocked by RCH worker clang-path mismatch and a long local frankenterm-core rebuild.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-oku4q","title":"mux dispatch swallows readable wait failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:18:25.203996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:31.725708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:31.725114Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1ad5c8cc (fix: connector_outbound_bridge zero-capacity, storage FTS rename, mux dispatch error propagation)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-olq2e","title":"[HIGH] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology never validates post-rollback topology","description":"Audit finding from commit 909c3d89.

File: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs:385-389

The test claims to verify that rollback restores the original topology, but it reconstructs `restored_topo` from `initial_topo_json` captured before mutation, not from the server/registry state after rollback:

- line 308 serializes the initial topology into `initial_topo_json`
- lines 385-389 deserialize that same baseline JSON and compare it to `initial_topo`

That assertion is vacuous: it will pass even if `RemoteRequest::Rollback` leaves the server in the wrong pane/tree layout, as long as the old serialized baseline is still parseable. The test currently proves JSON roundtrip of the baseline snapshot, not rollback correctness.

Expected fix:
- derive the post-rollback topology from the actual restored server state (or its durable checkpoint payload), then compare pane ids/tree structure against the pre-expansion baseline
- keep the current rollback-history assertions as secondary checks, not the only evidence

Regression risk: high false confidence on mux rollback behavior in the persistence lane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.010557Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:20.471789Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:20.471571Z","close_reason":"Phase 5 now derives post-rollback pane id set from server.registry().snapshot() instead of re-deserializing the pre-mutation baseline JSON. Primary assertion compares the live post-rollback pane id set against pre-expansion baseline; baseline JSON roundtrip retained as secondary serde check. All 3 tests in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs pass. Fix landed in 30172171.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,153 @@
+# Codec atomic-redeploy constraint
+
+**Bead:** [ft-og9bi](../.beads/issues.jsonl) (track A doc of ft-kuxho)
+**Related:** [ft-kuxho](../.beads/issues.jsonl) parent epic, [`docs/codec-versions.md`](codec-versions.md), [`docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md`](proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md), ft-nyvyl (slow-loris TLS handshake fix, ed413bd2)
+
+## TL;DR
+
+Today, **every `CODEC_VERSION` bump requires you to take down all mux
+servers and clients, then bring them back up at the new version, with
+no version skew tolerated in between.** This is a deployment
+constraint, not a transient bug — the wire format and the version
+field are both flat scalars and the codec has no negotiation. Until
+ft-kuxho/B's `CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED` window lands, schedule
+codec-bump deploys as maintenance windows and follow the runbook
+below.
+
+## 1. The failure mode
+
+Two facts compose into the constraint:
+
+1. **`CODEC_VERSION` is a single scalar.** `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:650`:
+
+   ```rust
+   pub const CODEC_VERSION: usize = 46;
+   ```
+
+   The handshake (`GetCodecVersion` / `GetCodecVersionResponse`, PDUs
+   26/27) returns one number. Existing client code compares with `==`
+   and aborts on mismatch — there is no version window, no
+   negotiation, no fallback path.
+
+2. **varbincode is a positional binary format.** Field offsets are
+   computed by serial position. Adding, removing, reordering, or
+   type-changing any field shifts every subsequent field's offset in
+   the encoded payload. The known-bad pattern documented in
+   `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:1204-1228` is `skip_serializing_if`
+   on `Option<T>`: it elides the tag byte for `None`, misaligning the
+   decoder for every downstream field.
+
+Combined: a v46 client decoding a v47 PDU sees a different field
+layout for any modified PDU and either:
+
+- decodes garbage values and proceeds — silent corruption, very rare
+  because length prefixes catch most cases;
+- hits a "failed to fill whole buffer" or
+  "tag byte out of range" decode error — the common case;
+- hits a buffer-overrun panic on a defensive `assert!` — the worst case
+  when the misalignment lands inside a length-prefixed `Vec`.
+
+There is no graceful fallback. The client either drops the PDU or
+crashes the connection.
+
+## 2. The long-lived TLS mux session caveat
+
+Production mux deployments use long-lived TLS sessions (per
+`crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/ossl.rs`; the slow-loris handshake
+window was bounded in ft-nyvyl / ed413bd2 but the *post-handshake*
+session is unlimited by design). Sessions stay open for hours; some
+agent panes hold a single connection for the lifetime of a workflow.
+
+This means:
+
+- **Rolling upgrades are not transparent.** Bumping the server to v47
+  while v46 clients are still connected does not migrate those
+  sessions — they continue to speak v46 because the handshake already
+  completed, but every new PDU shape change after the bump corrupts
+  their wire stream.
+- **Connection drops are the *correct* response.** Operators must
+  expect the server upgrade to terminate every active session.
+  Clients reconnect, re-handshake at v47, and resume. There is no
+  graceful-handoff path.
+- **In-flight workflow state survives connection drops** (the
+  recorder + checkpoint subsystem is independent of the wire
+  protocol), but any client-side cache of pane state, scrollback, or
+  pending RPCs is lost.
+
+## 3. Operator runbook for a `CODEC_VERSION` bump
+
+Until ft-kuxho/B's `CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED` window lands, follow
+this sequence for any deploy that includes a `CODEC_VERSION` bump.
+
+### 3a. Pre-deploy checklist
+
+- [ ] Confirm the bump is in the build by running `git diff main -- frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs` and verifying the new value.
+- [ ] Confirm the corresponding row exists in [`docs/codec-versions.md`](codec-versions.md). The CI guard `scripts/check_codec_version_release_notes.sh` (ft-8smkj) blocks merges that miss this, so reaching the deploy stage means it's there — but spot-check it in case of merge artifacts.
+- [ ] Schedule a maintenance window. Communicate the expected blast radius to operators of every workspace that has long-lived agent panes connected to a remote mux.
+- [ ] Pre-position the new client binaries on every workstation that will reconnect. The shorter the gap between server flip and client roll, the shorter the window of unavailability.
+
+### 3b. Deploy order — server first, then clients
+
+The strictly-correct order is:
+
+1. **Drain new connections to the old servers.** If you run multiple mux servers behind a load balancer, drain them one at a time. If single-server, skip to step 2.
+2. **Stop the old server.** Active sessions terminate; clients receive a connection drop.
+3. **Start the new server at v47.** Verify it accepts handshakes by attaching one v47 client.
+4. **Roll clients to v47.** Each client reconnects, handshakes at v47, and resumes. Until clients are rolled, they are not actually offline — they are crash-looping at handshake (server says 47, client says 46, both abort). That is the expected steady state during the rollout.
+5. **Verify zero v46 clients remain.** Check the mux server's structured logs for `codec_version=46` handshake-reject lines; they should taper to zero within the rollout window.
+
+### 3c. Cross-deploy coordination
+
+- **ft-zoxxq mux server** and **ft-nyvyl client** binaries must both be
+  bumped to the same `CODEC_VERSION` in the same release. The
+  ft-zoxxq.4 CI guard
+  (`scripts/check_mux_interface_imports.sh`) is independent of this
+  contract — it codifies the wezterm-fork stance for the trait
+  surface, not the wire version.
+- **Recorder/replay artifacts** are not affected. The recorder log
+  format is independent of the codec wire format; old recordings
+  remain decodable across `CODEC_VERSION` bumps.
+- **Workflows mid-execution** survive connection drops via the
+  recorder checkpoint subsystem. Operators should expect every
+  in-flight workflow to pause on the connection drop and resume on
+  reconnect; there is no data loss but there is a visible stall.
+
+### 3d. Rollback
+
+If the new build has a regression, roll back by reversing steps 3-5:
+stop the v47 server, start a v46 server, let the v47 clients fail
+their handshake until rolled back. Same pattern, opposite direction.
+
+## 4. The future relief valve
+
+[ft-kuxho/B](proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md)
+proposes a `CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED` constant that creates a
+backward-compat window: clients at any version
+`CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED <= v <= CODEC_VERSION` can interop. Once
+that lands:
+
+- **Additive bumps** (new PDU variant; new field with `serde(default)`
+  appended at the end of a struct — see ft-e1emx tail-padding
+  conformance harness at `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:3120+`) become
+  rolling-upgrade safe. `CODEC_VERSION` advances; `MIN` does not.
+  Old clients continue to decode the canonical prefix and ignore the
+  trailing extra bytes.
+- **Breaking bumps** still require the runbook above. Removing or
+  reordering fields, changing types, or inserting fields in the
+  middle of a struct all bump `MIN` and force atomic redeploy.
+
+The runbook in §3 should be retained for breaking bumps; the
+"maintenance window" overhead can be skipped for additive bumps once
+the window mechanism is in place. Until then, treat all bumps as
+breaking.
+
+## 5. Cross-references
+
+- [`docs/codec-versions.md`](codec-versions.md) — the version-history
+  release-note file enforced by ft-8smkj.
+- [`docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md`](proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md) — the design for the rolling-upgrade window.
+- `scripts/check_codec_version_release_notes.sh` — the CI guard that
+  prevents silent CODEC_VERSION bumps.
+- `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:650` — the `CODEC_VERSION` constant.
+- `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:1204-1228` — comments documenting the
+  varbincode positional-format hazard.

@@ -23,6 +23,12 @@ the same guard will be extended to require a row whenever `MIN`
 advances. Until then, every `CODEC_VERSION` bump implicitly raises the
 minimum to itself (atomic-redeploy semantics).
 
+**Operator note:** every entry in this table currently implies a
+maintenance-window deploy. See
+[`docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md`](codec-atomic-redeploy.md) for the
+deploy runbook (server-first ordering, expected connection drops,
+rollback procedure) and the rationale.
+
